evals.name: tea-shop-classic
evals.prompt: Map a small tea shop business that serves cups of tea to customers, needs to handle payment, and sources tea leaves from suppliers.
evals.expected_output: An OWM-format Wardley Map with a customer anchor, 5+ components, and a strategic analysis section naming Wardley gameplays.

assertions.text: Has a customer/user anchor at high visibility (>=0.9)
Visibility constraint respected for all edges
Cites at least one specific Wardley gameplay by name
Caveat present that evolution is scenario not forecast

evals.name: freelance-marketplace
evals.prompt: Create a Wardley Map for a freelance marketplace where clients post jobs, freelancers bid on them, and the platform takes a cut. Include a strategic analysis of where the platform should invest vs outsource.
evals.expected_output: An OWM-format Wardley Map with TWO anchors (client, freelancer), both at high visibility. Platform/matching infrastructure sits between them. Strategic analysis recognizes the two-sided dynamic.

assertions.text: Both anchors represent distinct roles (client and freelancer)
A platform/matching component exists as intermediate layer
Gives specific invest/outsource recommendations per component
Cites at least one specific Wardley gameplay by name

evals.name: saas-invoicing
evals.prompt: I'm building a SaaS for automated invoicing aimed at small businesses. Map the components and tell me what to build, buy, or outsource.
evals.expected_output: An OWM-format Wardley Map with 5+ components each with coordinates. Strategic analysis gives per-component build/buy/outsource calls and cites specific Wardley gameplays by name.

assertions.text: Cites at least one specific Wardley gameplay by name
Caveat present that evolution is scenario not forecast
